title: European Commission: ‘Guidance to Google for AI Interoperability on Android & Sharing of Google Search’
excerpt: What the EC is dictating to Google is just breathtaking in scope.
content_html:
The European Commission, last week:

Today, the European Commission has issued two sets of binding specification measures to Google under the Digital Markets Act.

The aim of the first specification measures is to ensure that competitors’ Artificial Intelligence (AI) services can compete with Google’s own AI services, such as Gemini, by having equal access to features on Google’s Android devices.

The aim of the second specification measures is to rebalance the playing field by giving third-party search engines access to search data that only Google Search can collect at scale.

They provide separate “Q&A” overviews of the guidance for Android AI interoperability and web search sharing, and the full guidance documents are PDFs (Case DMA.100220 for Android AI, Case DMA.100209 for web search). I suggest reading the two Q&A overviews, unless you’re having trouble falling asleep at night, in which case you’ll love the full PDF decisions.

Both decisions are interesting. With search, Google is required to share with competitors — search engines and AI chatbots alike — a massive amount of user data from Google Search user interactions. What terms people search for, what they click on in results, what languages and devices they use. It’s all ostensibly anonymized but that’s tricky when it comes to search terms. A lot of the terms people type into web search fields are to some degree personally identifying. The EC seems to be saying it’s Google’s problem to filter out things like passwords and usernames and omit them from the shared datasets. Google can charge money for this access, but only under “fair, reasonable, and non-discriminatory (FRAND)” prices, based on a Commission-defined methodology.

More interesting to me, however, is the guidance pertaining to on-device AI on Android devices. What the EC is dictating to Google is just breathtaking in scope. The EC is demanding that Google create APIs that allow third-party AI assistants to do everything Google Gemini does now, including:

  • Control hardware buttons (to invoke the assistant).
  • Capture anything on screen, from any app.
  • Seemingly unfettered access to microphones and cameras and other sensors on the device.
  • Unfettered background operation. Third-party AI assistants must be permitted to execute in the background whenever they want, for as long as they want.
  • Execute their own audio models on the digital signal processor, so they can listen at all times for their own custom “Hey Dingus” wake phrases/hot words.
  • Google must allow concurrent access to always-on hot word detection. So if you have Claude and ChatGPT and Grok and Meta AI installed, all of them — in addition to Gemini — must be permitted to have always-on audio detection concurrently. Google is permitted to do some vetting here, but this seems like madness.
  • Third-party models get access to Google’s on-device local models.

Also, in my reading, the EC is demanding that Google make available to third-party AI assistants all information in Google’s own apps (Gmail, Google Calendar, Google Docs, Google Maps, etc.) that Gemini has access to. There is no opt-out for Google regarding data from their own apps. Nor, I think, does this guidance allow third-party apps from other developers to only support specific system-level AI models. Like, let’s say you’re Slack, and you use the APIs to make the content from within Slack available to Gemini on the device. These guidelines don’t permit Google to allow Slack to say that they trust Gemini but only Gemini. If a third-party app like Slack supports making its data available to any system-level AI provider, it must make its data available to every system-level AI provider.

There’s a lot more. Basically, though, the EC is demanding that third-party AI assistants be enabled to become part of the system software, not just apps. I’m sure some people think this is a great idea. It’s the user’s device, they should be allowed to make ChatGPT or Claude or Meta AI part of their OS if they want. It’s up to them. Put users in control.

This is how PCs have traditionally worked, but many normal people’s PCs are a mess of third-party software running in the background. That includes the Mac. If you ask a normal person “What third-party software runs in the background on your Mac or PC?” they would have no idea. It’s all just magic to them. If Google supports this guidance, it could turn Android phones in the EU into PCs. Honestly, some of the stuff the EC is requiring is lower-level than what MacOS and Windows allow third-party software to do.

What the EC’s “guidance” describes in this document is an entirely different operating system than the Android that Google has designed. The European Commission obviously thinks it is their place to design operating systems. Maybe you do too. Google obviously disagrees, and so does Apple. And so should most people who have any idea how these devices work. This is a recipe for disaster, if Google were to enact it and third-party AI assistants took advantage of it.

That second “if” is a big one. One possible scenario that I consider quite likely is that Google could spend years of engineering time and human resources building out APIs to enable all of this, in the safest and most private ways possible, and no major AI assistant adopts it. That’s the way it’s turned out with a whole slew of DMA compliance for Apple and Google. Apple built an entire complex set of APIs to enable third-party web browser rendering engines, exclusively for compliance with the DMA, and there exist no third-party web browser rendering engines for iOS. Not one. Because while the EU is a big market, it’s not big enough to justify building a custom web browser just for the EU alone.

Ways I can see this playing out, in order of likelihood:

(A) Google enacts all of this and no major AI assistants support it because it’s only for Android, only in the EU. And it’s not like ChatGPT and Claude are seeing a lack of usage as things stand now. In this scenario Google just wastes massive time and engineering talent building APIs that never get used, and Android users in the EU get hassled with additional annoying choice and permission screens just to use the Gemini features that are built into Android.

(B) Google enacts all of this and major AI assistants do support it. Unintended results include massive privacy violations where third-party assistants exfiltrate on-device data to the cloud, Meta uses on-device third-party data for the targeting of ads, and users who take advantage of these third-party assistants see significant battery life drain as third-party assistants run without limits in the background and run expensive inference locally to save on their own server costs.

(C) Google enacts all of this, major AI assistants do support it, and there are no privacy scandals, nor any issues with battery life, because each of the companies that makes these assistants develops them with respect for user privacy and for device resources like CPU and memory consumption.

(D) Google pulls system-integrated Gemini from Android in the EU, or severely restricts its capabilities. Rather than elevate third-party assistants from apps to system software, demote Gemini to the privileges of a mere app and leave Android users in the EU without a system-integrated AI assistant.

Under all scenarios, future feature updates to system-level AI in Android will appear late or never in the EU. No future new features can debut in the EU at the same time as the rest of the world because for DMA compliance, Google will need to add support for third-party assistants to do the same things. And they’re not going to hold new features for the rest of the world waiting for that.

I’m not even sure (D) is permitted under this Commission guidance, given that Google started shipping Gemini on Android last year. The entire guidance document is written under the presumption that Google will comply by building the APIs that the guidance demands, not by achieving parity by removing Gemini system integration features. It would be awkward and unpopular for Google to ship updates to Android that remove core AI features, but that might be more palatable than the alternative.1 Note that with iOS, to my recollection, Apple hasn’t pulled any existing features from the EU. They’ve only withheld or delayed new features. Google’s on-device Gemini horse is already out of the barn.

Lastly, although this guidance document pertains to Android, not iOS, I see no reason to think the European Commission wouldn’t demand all or most of the same things from Apple. They haven’t given Apple any guidance yet, because it’s European Commission policy to give guidance only after a DMA-designator gatekeeper ships something that is then ruled non-compliant. But it’s hard to imagine Apple accepting most of these terms. Unfettered background processing and access to the microphone, cameras, and sensors? Third-party audio models running on the hardware DSP listening for wake words? Granting third-party assistants unsupervised access to all user data from apps published using App Intents?

Apple unfortunately hasn’t shared any technical details describing its proposal for a “Trusted System Agent” that it shared with the EC last year. But whatever Apple’s vision for the Trusted System Agent is, I don’t see how the EC would deem it compliant with the DMA if they want from Apple anything close to what they are now demanding from Google. There are no checks and balances or oversight that Google is permitted to apply to third-party assistants in this guidance. If Gemini can do something, third-party agents must be able to as well. Because Gemini gets to run in the background as much as Google sees fit, third-party assistants must be permitted to run in the background as much as they see fit. And if those third-party assistant developers — OpenAI, Anthropic, xAI, and Meta — have different opinions than Google on how much CPU usage is appropriate in the background, how much RAM and storage is appropriate to consume, or how respectfully to treat users’ on-device data, well that’s just tough noogies. If the user OK’s it, then it’s OK.

A month ago, after WWDC, when this guidance pertaining to Android AI was rumored to be forthcoming, I wrote:

Google is learning the lesson Apple learned the hard way with all the existing features of iOS that were deemed noncompliant with the DMA when it went into effect. The “ship it first and ask forgiveness / hope it’s deemed compliant” strategy is not a good one in the EU.

I genuinely wonder what the European Commission thinks the purpose of Android is. Google created Android for the benefit of its own services. Google was worried about Microsoft, not Apple, at the time, but they wanted to ensure their own services were available on a major mobile platform. I’m really not seeing how it’s more attractive to Google to comply with this guidance than to just pull system-level Gemini in the EU. Either they waste a small fortune building APIs no one will use, or, they spend that small fortune buildings APIs for the benefit of their biggest competitors. I get it that that’s the intended price to pay for being a designated DMA gatekeeper. But what’s the motivation for Google to do this rather than just walk away from system-integrated AI on Android in the EU? It certainly doesn’t look like the competing platform, iOS, is going to offer it in the EU anytime soon either.

  1. If you are handed a mandate that everyone must be able to run at the same speed, and you can’t figure out how to make slow people faster, you can comply by forcing the fast to wear weighted boots. This is where utopian egalitarian initiatives often lead. ↩︎

sentences.en: Third-party AI assistants must be permitted to execute in the background whenever they want, for as long as they want.
sentences.zh: 必须允许第三方人工智能助手在后台随时执行、执行任意时间。

blocks.html_en: • Execute their own audio models on the digital signal processor, so they can listen at all times for their own custom “Hey Dingus” wake phrases/hot words.

sentences.en: Execute their own audio models on the digital signal processor, so they can listen at all times for their own custom “Hey Dingus” wake phrases/hot words.
sentences.zh: 在数字信号处理器上执行自己的音频模型，这样他们就可以随时收听自己定制的“Hey Dingus”唤醒短语/热词。

blocks.html_en: • Google must allow concurrent access to always-on hot word detection. So if you have Claude and ChatGPT and Grok and Meta AI installed, all of them — in addition to Gemini — must be permitted to have always-on audio detection concurrently. Google is permitted to do some vetting here, but this seems like madness.

sentences.en: Google must allow concurrent access to always-on hot word detection.
sentences.zh: Google 必须允许并发访问始终在线的热词检测。

sentences.en: So if you have Claude and ChatGPT and Grok and Meta AI installed, all of them — in addition to Gemini — must be permitted to have always-on audio detection concurrently.
sentences.zh: 因此，如果您安装了 Claude 和 ChatGPT、Grok 和 Meta AI，那么除了 Gemini 之外，所有这些都必须被允许同时进行始终在线的音频检测。

sentences.en: Google is permitted to do some vetting here, but this seems like madness.
sentences.zh: 谷歌被允许在这里进行一些审查，但这看起来很疯狂。

blocks.html_en: • Third-party models get access to Google’s on-device local models.

sentences.en: Third-party models get access to Google’s on-device local models.
sentences.zh: 第三方模型可以访问 Google 设备上的本地模型。

blocks.html_en: Also, in my reading, the EC is demanding that Google make available to third-party AI assistants all information in Google’s own apps (Gmail, Google Calendar, Google Docs, Google Maps, etc.) that Gemini has access to. There is no opt-out for Google regarding data from their own apps. Nor, I think, does this guidance allow third-party apps from other developers to only support specific system-level AI models. Like, let’s say you’re Slack, and you use the APIs to make the content from within Slack available to Gemini on the device. These guidelines don’t permit Google to allow Slack to say that they trust Gemini but only Gemini. If a third-party app like Slack supports making its data available to any system-level AI provider, it must make its data available to every system-level AI provider.

sentences.en: Also, in my reading, the EC is demanding that Google make available to third-party AI assistants all information in Google’s own apps (Gmail, Google Calendar, Google Docs, Google Maps, etc.) that Gemini has access to.
sentences.zh: 此外，在我的阅读中，欧盟委员会要求谷歌向第三方人工智能助手提供 Gemini 可以访问的谷歌自己的应用程序（Gmail、谷歌日历、谷歌文档、谷歌地图等）中的所有信息。

sentences.en: There is no opt-out for Google regarding data from their own apps.
sentences.zh: 对于来自自己应用程序的数据，谷歌没有选择退出的权利。

sentences.en: Nor, I think, does this guidance allow third-party apps from other developers to only support specific system-level AI models.
sentences.zh: 我认为，该指南也不允许其他开发人员的第三方应用程序仅支持特定的系统级人工智能模型。

sentences.en: Like, let’s say you’re Slack, and you use the APIs to make the content from within Slack available to Gemini on the device.
sentences.zh: 例如，假设您是 Slack，并且您使用 API 使设备上的 Gemini 可以使用 Slack 中的内容。

sentences.en: These guidelines don’t permit Google to allow Slack to say that they trust Gemini but only Gemini.
sentences.zh: 这些准则不允许 Google 允许 Slack 说他们信任 Gemini，但只信任 Gemini。

sentences.en: If a third-party app like Slack supports making its data available to any system-level AI provider, it must make its data available to every system-level AI provider.
sentences.zh: 如果像 Slack 这样的第三方应用程序支持将其数据提供给任何系统级 AI 提供商，则它必须将其数据提供给每个系统级 AI 提供商。

blocks.html_en: There’s a lot more. Basically, though, the EC is demanding that third-party AI assistants be enabled to become part of the system software, not just apps. I’m sure some people think this is a great idea. It’s the user’s device, they should be allowed to make ChatGPT or Claude or Meta AI part of their OS if they want. It’s up to them. Put users in control.

sentences.en: There’s a lot more.
sentences.zh: 还有更多。

sentences.en: Basically, though, the EC is demanding that third-party AI assistants be enabled to become part of the system software, not just apps.
sentences.zh: 不过，基本上，欧盟委员会要求第三方人工智能助手成为系统软件的一部分，而不仅仅是应用程序。

sentences.en: I’m sure some people think this is a great idea.
sentences.zh: 我确信有些人认为这是个好主意。

sentences.en: It’s the user’s device, they should be allowed to make ChatGPT or Claude or Meta AI part of their OS if they want.
sentences.zh: 这是用户的设备，如果他们愿意，应该允许他们将 ChatGPT 或 Claude 或 Meta AI 作为其操作系统的一部分。

sentences.en: It’s up to them.
sentences.zh: 这取决于他们。

sentences.en: Put users in control.
sentences.zh: 让用户掌控。

blocks.html_en: This is how PCs have traditionally worked, but many normal people’s PCs are a mess of third-party software running in the background. That includes the Mac. If you ask a normal person “What third-party software runs in the background on your Mac or PC?” they would have no idea. It’s all just magic to them. If Google supports this guidance, it could turn Android phones in the EU into PCs. Honestly, some of the stuff the EC is requiring is lower-level than what MacOS and Windows allow third-party software to do.

sentences.en: This is how PCs have traditionally worked, but many normal people’s PCs are a mess of third-party software running in the background.
sentences.zh: 这就是 PC 传统上的工作方式，但许多普通人的 PC 都是在后台运行的一堆第三方软件。

sentences.en: That includes the Mac.
sentences.zh: 其中包括 Mac。

sentences.en: If you ask a normal person “What third-party software runs in the background on your Mac or PC?”
sentences.zh: 如果你问一个普通人“你的 Mac 或 PC 上后台运行哪些第三方软件？”

sentences.en: they would have no idea.
sentences.zh: 他们不知道。

sentences.en: It’s all just magic to them.
sentences.zh: 这对他们来说简直就是魔法。

sentences.en: If Google supports this guidance, it could turn Android phones in the EU into PCs.
sentences.zh: 如果谷歌支持这一指导，它可以将欧盟的安卓手机变成个人电脑。

sentences.en: Honestly, some of the stuff the EC is requiring is lower-level than what MacOS and Windows allow third-party software to do.
sentences.zh: 老实说，EC 要求的某些功能比 MacOS 和 Windows 允许第三方软件执行的功能要低。

blocks.html_en: What the EC’s “guidance” describes in this document is an entirely different operating system than the Android that Google has designed. The European Commission obviously thinks it is their place to design operating systems. Maybe you do too. Google obviously disagrees, and so does Apple. And so should most people who have any idea how these devices work. This is a recipe for disaster, if Google were to enact it and third-party AI assistants took advantage of it.

sentences.en: What the EC’s “guidance” describes in this document is an entirely different operating system than the Android that Google has designed.
sentences.zh: EC的“指南”在本文件中描述的是与谷歌设计的Android完全不同的操作系统。

sentences.en: The European Commission obviously thinks it is their place to design operating systems.
sentences.zh: 欧盟委员会显然认为他们应该设计操作系统。

sentences.en: Maybe you do too.
sentences.zh: 也许你也是。

sentences.en: Google obviously disagrees, and so does Apple.
sentences.zh: 谷歌显然不同意，苹果也不同意。

sentences.en: And so should most people who have any idea how these devices work.
sentences.zh: 大多数了解这些设备如何工作的人也应该如此。

sentences.en: This is a recipe for disaster, if Google were to enact it and third-party AI assistants took advantage of it.
sentences.zh: 如果谷歌实施这一政策并且第三方人工智能助手利用了它，这将酿成灾难。

blocks.html_en: That second “if” is a big one. One possible scenario that I consider quite likely is that Google could spend years of engineering time and human resources building out APIs to enable all of this, in the safest and most private ways possible, and no major AI assistant adopts it. That’s the way it’s turned out with a whole slew of DMA compliance for Apple and Google. Apple built an entire complex set of APIs to enable third-party web browser rendering engines, exclusively for compliance with the DMA, and there exist no third-party web browser rendering engines for iOS. Not one. Because while the EU is a big market, it’s not big enough to justify building a custom web browser just for the EU alone.

sentences.en: That second “if” is a big one.
sentences.zh: 第二个“如果”是一个很大的问题。

sentences.en: One possible scenario that I consider quite likely is that Google could spend years of engineering time and human resources building out APIs to enable all of this, in the safest and most private ways possible, and no major AI assistant adopts it.
sentences.zh: 我认为很可能发生的一种情况是，谷歌可能会花费数年的工程时间和人力资源来构建 API，以尽可能最安全和最私密的方式实现所有这一切，并且没有主要的人工智能助手采用它。

sentences.en: That’s the way it’s turned out with a whole slew of DMA compliance for Apple and Google.
sentences.zh: 这就是苹果和谷歌大量 DMA 合规性的结果。

sentences.en: Apple built an entire complex set of APIs to enable third-party web browser rendering engines, exclusively for compliance with the DMA, and there exist no third-party web browser rendering engines for iOS.
sentences.zh: Apple 构建了一整套复杂的 API 来启用第三方 Web 浏览器渲染引擎，专门用于遵守 DMA，并且不存在适用于 iOS 的第三方 Web 浏览器渲染引擎。

sentences.en: Not one.
sentences.zh: 没有一个。

sentences.en: Because while the EU is a big market, it’s not big enough to justify building a custom web browser just for the EU alone.
sentences.zh: 因为虽然欧盟是一个很大的市场，但它还不够大，不足以证明只为欧盟构建自定义网络浏览器是合理的。

blocks.html_en: Ways I can see this playing out, in order of likelihood:

sentences.en: Ways I can see this playing out, in order of likelihood:
sentences.zh: 我可以看到这种情况发生的方式，按可能性的顺序：

blocks.html_en: (A) Google enacts all of this and no major AI assistants support it because it’s only for Android, only in the EU. And it’s not like ChatGPT and Claude are seeing a lack of usage as things stand now. In this scenario Google just wastes massive time and engineering talent building APIs that never get used, and Android users in the EU get hassled with additional annoying choice and permission screens just to use the Gemini features that are built into Android.

sentences.en: (A) Google enacts all of this and no major AI assistants support it because it’s only for Android, only in the EU.
sentences.zh: (A) 谷歌制定了所有这些，但没有主要的人工智能助手支持它，因为它仅适用于 Android，仅在欧盟。

sentences.en: And it’s not like ChatGPT and Claude are seeing a lack of usage as things stand now.
sentences.zh: ChatGPT 和 Claude 并没有看到目前的情况缺乏使用。

sentences.en: In this scenario Google just wastes massive time and engineering talent building APIs that never get used, and Android users in the EU get hassled with additional annoying choice and permission screens just to use the Gemini features that are built into Android.
sentences.zh: 在这种情况下，谷歌只是浪费了大量的时间和工程人才来构建从未使用过的 API，而欧盟的 Android 用户只是为了使用 Android 内置的 Gemini 功能，就会遇到额外烦人的选择和权限屏幕的麻烦。

blocks.html_en: (B) Google enacts all of this and major AI assistants do support it. Unintended results include massive privacy violations where third-party assistants exfiltrate on-device data to the cloud, Meta uses on-device third-party data for the targeting of ads, and users who take advantage of these third-party assistants see significant battery life drain as third-party assistants run without limits in the background and run expensive inference locally to save on their own server costs.

sentences.en: (B) Google enacts all of this and major AI assistants do support it.
sentences.zh: (B) 谷歌制定了所有这些，并且主要的人工智能助手确实支持它。

sentences.en: Unintended results include massive privacy violations where third-party assistants exfiltrate on-device data to the cloud, Meta uses on-device third-party data for the targeting of ads, and users who take advantage of these third-party assistants see significant battery life drain as third-party assistants run without limits in the background and run expensive inference locally to save on their own server costs.
sentences.zh: 意想不到的结果包括大规模的隐私侵犯，其中第三方助手将设备上的数据泄露到云端，Meta 使用设备上的第三方数据来定位广告，而利用这些第三方助手的用户会发现电池寿命严重耗尽，因为第三方助手在后台无限制地运行，并在本地运行昂贵的推理以节省自己的服务器成本。

blocks.html_en: (C) Google enacts all of this, major AI assistants do support it, and there are no privacy scandals, nor any issues with battery life, because each of the companies that makes these assistants develops them with respect for user privacy and for device resources like CPU and memory consumption.

sentences.en: (C) Google enacts all of this, major AI assistants do support it, and there are no privacy scandals, nor any issues with battery life, because each of the companies that makes these assistants develops them with respect for user privacy and for device resources like CPU and memory consumption.
sentences.zh: (C) 谷歌制定了所有这些，主要的人工智能助手确实支持它，并且不存在隐私丑闻，也不存在任何电池寿命问题，因为制造这些助手的每家公司在开发它们时都尊重用户隐私以及 CPU 和内存消耗等设备资源。

blocks.html_en: (D) Google pulls system-integrated Gemini from Android in the EU, or severely restricts its capabilities. Rather than elevate third-party assistants from apps to system software, demote Gemini to the privileges of a mere app and leave Android users in the EU without a system-integrated AI assistant.

sentences.en: (D) Google pulls system-integrated Gemini from Android in the EU, or severely restricts its capabilities.
sentences.zh: (D) 谷歌在欧盟将系统集成的 Gemini 从 Android 中撤下，或严格限制其功能。

sentences.en: Rather than elevate third-party assistants from apps to system software, demote Gemini to the privileges of a mere app and leave Android users in the EU without a system-integrated AI assistant.
sentences.zh: 与其将第三方助手从应用程序提升为系统软件，不如将 Gemini 降级为单纯应用程序的特权，让欧盟的 Android 用户无法获得系统集成的人工智能助手。

blocks.html_en: Under all scenarios, future feature updates to system-level AI in Android will appear late or never in the EU. No future new features can debut in the EU at the same time as the rest of the world because for DMA compliance, Google will need to add support for third-party assistants to do the same things. And they’re not going to hold new features for the rest of the world waiting for that.

sentences.en: Under all scenarios, future feature updates to system-level AI in Android will appear late or never in the EU.
sentences.zh: 在所有情况下，Android 系统级人工智能的未来功能更新在欧盟都将出现较晚或永远不会出现。

sentences.en: No future new features can debut in the EU at the same time as the rest of the world because for DMA compliance, Google will need to add support for third-party assistants to do the same things.
sentences.zh: 未来的新功能不可能与世界其他地区同时在欧盟推出，因为为了符合 DMA 合规性，谷歌将需要添加对第三方助手的支持来完成同样的事情。

sentences.en: And they’re not going to hold new features for the rest of the world waiting for that.
sentences.zh: 他们不会为等待的世界其他地方提供新功能。

blocks.html_en: I’m not even sure (D) is permitted under this Commission guidance, given that Google started shipping Gemini on Android last year. The entire guidance document is written under the presumption that Google will comply by building the APIs that the guidance demands, not by achieving parity by removing Gemini system integration features. It would be awkward and unpopular for Google to ship updates to Android that remove core AI features, but that might be more palatable than the alternative.1 Note that with iOS, to my recollection, Apple hasn’t pulled any existing features from the EU. They’ve only withheld or delayed new features. Google’s on-device Gemini horse is already out of the barn.

sentences.en: I’m not even sure (D) is permitted under this Commission guidance, given that Google started shipping Gemini on Android last year.
sentences.zh: 鉴于谷歌去年开始在 Android 上发布 Gemini，我什至不确定委员会的指导是否允许 (D)。

sentences.en: The entire guidance document is written under the presumption that Google will comply by building the APIs that the guidance demands, not by achieving parity by removing Gemini system integration features.
sentences.zh: 整个指导文件是在这样的假设下编写的：Google 将通过构建指导要求的 API 来遵守，而不是通过删除 Gemini 系统集成功能来实现对等。

sentences.en: It would be awkward and unpopular for Google to ship updates to Android that remove core AI features, but that might be more palatable than the alternative.1 Note that with iOS, to my recollection, Apple hasn’t pulled any existing features from the EU.
sentences.zh: 对于谷歌来说，向 Android 发布删除核心 AI 功能的更新会很尴尬，也不受欢迎，但这可能比替代方案更容易接受。1 请注意，据我记得，对于 iOS，苹果尚未从欧盟撤回任何现有功能。

sentences.en: They’ve only withheld or delayed new features.
sentences.zh: 他们只是拒绝或推迟了新功能。

sentences.en: Google’s on-device Gemini horse is already out of the barn.
sentences.zh: 谷歌设备上的双子座马已经走出谷仓。

blocks.html_en: Lastly, although this guidance document pertains to Android, not iOS, I see no reason to think the European Commission wouldn’t demand all or most of the same things from Apple. They haven’t given Apple any guidance yet, because it’s European Commission policy to give guidance only after a DMA-designator gatekeeper ships something that is then ruled non-compliant. But it’s hard to imagine Apple accepting most of these terms. Unfettered background processing and access to the microphone, cameras, and sensors? Third-party audio models running on the hardware DSP listening for wake words? Granting third-party assistants unsupervised access to all user data from apps published using App Intents?

sentences.en: Lastly, although this guidance document pertains to Android, not iOS, I see no reason to think the European Commission wouldn’t demand all or most of the same things from Apple.
sentences.zh: 最后，虽然这份指导文件适用于 Android，而不是 iOS，但我认为欧盟委员会没有理由不要求 Apple 提供全部或大部分相同的东西。

sentences.en: They haven’t given Apple any guidance yet, because it’s European Commission policy to give guidance only after a DMA-designator gatekeeper ships something that is then ruled non-compliant.
sentences.zh: 他们尚未向苹果提供任何指导，因为欧盟委员会的政策是，只有在 DMA 指定看门人运送了被裁定不合规的产品后才提供指导。

sentences.en: But it’s hard to imagine Apple accepting most of these terms.
sentences.zh: 但很难想象苹果会接受其中大部分条款。

sentences.en: Unfettered background processing and access to the microphone, cameras, and sensors?
sentences.zh: 不受限制的后台处理和对麦克风、摄像头和传感器的访问？

sentences.en: Third-party audio models running on the hardware DSP listening for wake words?
sentences.zh: 在硬件 DSP 上运行的第三方音频模型监听唤醒词？

sentences.en: Granting third-party assistants unsupervised access to all user data from apps published using App Intents?
sentences.zh: 授予第三方助手不受监督地访问使用应用程序意图发布的应用程序中的所有用户数据？

blocks.html_en: Apple unfortunately hasn’t shared any technical details describing its proposal for a “Trusted System Agent” that it shared with the EC last year. But whatever Apple’s vision for the Trusted System Agent is, I don’t see how the EC would deem it compliant with the DMA if they want from Apple anything close to what they are now demanding from Google. There are no checks and balances or oversight that Google is permitted to apply to third-party assistants in this guidance. If Gemini can do something, third-party agents must be able to as well. Because Gemini gets to run in the background as much as Google sees fit, third-party assistants must be permitted to run in the background as much as they see fit. And if those third-party assistant developers — OpenAI, Anthropic, xAI, and Meta — have different opinions than Google on how much CPU usage is appropriate in the background, how much RAM and storage is appropriate to consume, or how respectfully to treat users’ on-device data, well that’s just tough noogies. If the user OK’s it, then it’s OK.

sentences.en: Apple unfortunately hasn’t shared any technical details describing its proposal for a “Trusted System Agent” that it shared with the EC last year.
sentences.zh: 不幸的是，苹果公司尚未分享任何描述其去年与欧盟委员会分享的“可信系统代理”提案的技术细节。

sentences.en: But whatever Apple’s vision for the Trusted System Agent is, I don’t see how the EC would deem it compliant with the DMA if they want from Apple anything close to what they are now demanding from Google.
sentences.zh: 但无论 Apple 对可信系统代理的愿景是什么，如果 EC 想要从 Apple 获得与他们现在对 Google 的要求接近的东西，我不知道 EC 将如何认为它符合 DMA。

sentences.en: There are no checks and balances or oversight that Google is permitted to apply to third-party assistants in this guidance.
sentences.zh: 在本指南中，Google 没有被允许对第三方助手进行任何制衡或监督。

sentences.en: If Gemini can do something, third-party agents must be able to as well.
sentences.zh: 如果 Gemini 能做某事，那么第三方代理也一定能做。

sentences.en: Because Gemini gets to run in the background as much as Google sees fit, third-party assistants must be permitted to run in the background as much as they see fit.
sentences.zh: 由于 Gemini 可以在 Google 认为合适的情况下在后台运行，因此必须允许第三方助手在他们认为合适的情况下在后台运行。

sentences.en: And if those third-party assistant developers — OpenAI, Anthropic, xAI, and Meta — have different opinions than Google on how much CPU usage is appropriate in the background, how much RAM and storage is appropriate to consume, or how respectfully to treat users’ on-device data, well that’s just tough noogies.
sentences.zh: 如果那些第三方辅助开发者——OpenAI、Anthropic、xAI 和 Meta——在后台使用多少 CPU 合适、消耗多少 RAM 和存储、或者如何尊重用户设备上的数据方面与 Google 有不同的看法，那么这只是棘手的问题。

sentences.en: If the user OK’s it, then it’s OK.
sentences.zh: 如果用户同意，那就可以。

blocks.html_en: A month ago, after WWDC, when this guidance pertaining to Android AI was rumored to be forthcoming, I wrote:

sentences.en: A month ago, after WWDC, when this guidance pertaining to Android AI was rumored to be forthcoming, I wrote:
sentences.zh: 一个月前，WWDC 结束后，当传言有关 Android AI 的指南即将发布时，我写道：

blocks.html_en: Google is learning the lesson Apple learned the hard way with all the existing features of iOS that were deemed noncompliant with the DMA when it went into effect. The “ship it first and ask forgiveness / hope it’s deemed compliant” strategy is not a good one in the EU.

sentences.en: Google is learning the lesson Apple learned the hard way with all the existing features of iOS that were deemed noncompliant with the DMA when it went into effect.
sentences.zh: 谷歌正在汲取苹果公司惨痛教训的教训，iOS 的所有现有功能在 DMA 生效时均被视为不符合规定。

sentences.en: The “ship it first and ask forgiveness / hope it’s deemed compliant” strategy is not a good one in the EU.
sentences.zh: “先发货并请求宽恕/希望它被视为合规”的策略在欧盟并不是一个好的策略。

blocks.html_en: Google is learning the lesson Apple learned the hard way with all the existing features of iOS that were deemed noncompliant with the DMA when it went into effect. The “ship it first and ask forgiveness / hope it’s deemed compliant” strategy is not a good one in the EU.

sentences.en: Google is learning the lesson Apple learned the hard way with all the existing features of iOS that were deemed noncompliant with the DMA when it went into effect.
sentences.zh: 谷歌正在汲取苹果公司惨痛教训的教训，iOS 的所有现有功能在 DMA 生效时均被视为不符合规定。

sentences.en: The “ship it first and ask forgiveness / hope it’s deemed compliant” strategy is not a good one in the EU.
sentences.zh: “先发货并请求宽恕/希望它被视为合规”的策略在欧盟并不是一个好的策略。

blocks.html_en: I genuinely wonder what the European Commission thinks the purpose of Android is. Google created Android for the benefit of its own services. Google was worried about Microsoft, not Apple, at the time, but they wanted to ensure their own services were available on a major mobile platform. I’m really not seeing how it’s more attractive to Google to comply with this guidance than to just pull system-level Gemini in the EU. Either they waste a small fortune building APIs no one will use, or, they spend that small fortune buildings APIs for the benefit of their biggest competitors. I get it that that’s the intended price to pay for being a designated DMA gatekeeper. But what’s the motivation for Google to do this rather than just walk away from system-integrated AI on Android in the EU? It certainly doesn’t look like the competing platform, iOS, is going to offer it in the EU anytime soon either.

sentences.zh: 我真的不明白遵守这一指导方针对谷歌来说比仅仅将系统级 Gemini 引入欧盟更具吸引力。

sentences.en: I get it that that’s the intended price to pay for being a designated DMA gatekeeper.
sentences.zh: 我知道这是成为指定 DMA 看门人的预期价格。

content_html_zh:
欧盟委员会上周：

今天，欧盟委员会根据《数字市场法》向谷歌发布了两套具有约束力的规范措施。 第一项规范措施的目的是确保竞争对手的人工智能（AI）服务能够通过平等地访问谷歌Android设备上的功能来与谷歌自己的人工智能服务（例如Gemini）竞争。 第二项规范措施的目的是让第三方搜索引擎能够访问只有 Google 搜索才能大规模收集的搜索数据，从而重新平衡竞争环境。

他们提供了有关 Android AI 互操作性和 Web 搜索共享指南的单独“问答”概述，完整的指南文档为 PDF（适用于 Android AI 的 Case DMA.100220，适用于 Web 搜索的 Case DMA.100209）。 我建议您阅读两个问答概述，除非您晚上难以入睡，在这种情况下您会喜欢完整的 PDF 决策。

这两个决定都很有趣。 在搜索方面，谷歌需要与竞争对手（搜索引擎和人工智能聊天机器人等）共享来自谷歌搜索用户交互的大量用户数据。 人们搜索什么术语、在结果中点击什么、使用什么语言和设备。 表面上都是匿名的，但对于搜索词来说这很棘手。 人们在网络搜索字段中输入的许多术语在某种程度上都是个人识别的。 欧盟委员会似乎在说，过滤掉密码和用户名等内容并将其从共享数据集中忽略是谷歌的问题。 谷歌可以针对这种访问收取费用，但只能根据委员会定义的方法以“公平、合理和非歧视（FRAND）”的价格收费。

然而，对我来说更有趣的是有关 Android 设备上的设备上人工智能的指南。 欧盟委员会向谷歌发出的指令范围之广令人惊叹。 欧盟委员会要求谷歌创建 API，允许第三方人工智能助手完成 Google Gemini 现在所做的一切，包括：

  • 控制硬件按钮（以调用助手）。
  • 从任何应用程序捕获屏幕上的任何内容。
  • 看似不受限制地访问设备上的麦克风、摄像头和其他传感器。
  • 不受限制的后台操作。 必须允许第三方人工智能助手在后台随时执行、执行任意时间。
  • 在数字信号处理器上执行自己的音频模型，这样他们就可以随时收听自己定制的“Hey Dingus”唤醒短语/热词。
  • Google 必须允许并发访问始终在线的热词检测。 因此，如果您安装了 Claude 和 ChatGPT、Grok 和 Meta AI，那么除了 Gemini 之外，所有这些都必须被允许同时进行始终在线的音频检测。 谷歌被允许在这里进行一些审查，但这看起来很疯狂。
  • 第三方模型可以访问 Google 设备上的本地模型。

此外，在我的阅读中，欧盟委员会要求谷歌向第三方人工智能助手提供 Gemini 可以访问的谷歌自己的应用程序（Gmail、谷歌日历、谷歌文档、谷歌地图等）中的所有信息。 对于来自自己应用程序的数据，谷歌没有选择退出的权利。 我认为，该指南也不允许其他开发人员的第三方应用程序仅支持特定的系统级人工智能模型。 例如，假设您是 Slack，并且您使用 API 使设备上的 Gemini 可以使用 Slack 中的内容。 这些准则不允许 Google 允许 Slack 说他们信任 Gemini，但只信任 Gemini。 如果像 Slack 这样的第三方应用程序支持将其数据提供给任何系统级 AI 提供商，则它必须将其数据提供给每个系统级 AI 提供商。

还有更多。 不过，基本上，欧盟委员会要求第三方人工智能助手成为系统软件的一部分，而不仅仅是应用程序。 我确信有些人认为这是个好主意。 这是用户的设备，如果他们愿意，应该允许他们将 ChatGPT 或 Claude 或 Meta AI 作为其操作系统的一部分。 这取决于他们。 让用户掌控。

这就是 PC 传统上的工作方式，但许多普通人的 PC 都是在后台运行的一堆第三方软件。 其中包括 Mac。 如果你问一个普通人“你的 Mac 或 PC 上后台运行哪些第三方软件？” 他们不知道。 这对他们来说简直就是魔法。 如果谷歌支持这一指导，它可以将欧盟的安卓手机变成个人电脑。 老实说，EC 要求的某些功能比 MacOS 和 Windows 允许第三方软件执行的功能要低。

EC的“指南”在本文件中描述的是与谷歌设计的Android完全不同的操作系统。 欧盟委员会显然认为他们应该设计操作系统。 也许你也是。 谷歌显然不同意，苹果也不同意。 大多数了解这些设备如何工作的人也应该如此。 如果谷歌实施这一政策并且第三方人工智能助手利用了它，这将酿成灾难。

第二个“如果”是一个很大的问题。 我认为很可能发生的一种情况是，谷歌可能会花费数年的工程时间和人力资源来构建 API，以尽可能最安全和最私密的方式实现所有这一切，并且没有主要的人工智能助手采用它。 这就是苹果和谷歌大量 DMA 合规性的结果。 Apple 构建了一整套复杂的 API 来启用第三方 Web 浏览器渲染引擎，专门用于遵守 DMA，并且不存在适用于 iOS 的第三方 Web 浏览器渲染引擎。 没有一个。 因为虽然欧盟是一个很大的市场，但它还不够大，不足以证明只为欧盟构建自定义网络浏览器是合理的。

我可以看到这种情况发生的方式，按可能性的顺序：

(A) 谷歌制定了所有这些，但没有主要的人工智能助手支持它，因为它仅适用于 Android，仅在欧盟。 ChatGPT 和 Claude 并没有看到目前的情况缺乏使用。 在这种情况下，谷歌只是浪费了大量的时间和工程人才来构建从未使用过的 API，而欧盟的 Android 用户只是为了使用 Android 内置的 Gemini 功能，就会遇到额外烦人的选择和权限屏幕的麻烦。

(B) 谷歌制定了所有这些，并且主要的人工智能助手确实支持它。 意想不到的结果包括大规模的隐私侵犯，其中第三方助手将设备上的数据泄露到云端，Meta 使用设备上的第三方数据来定位广告，而利用这些第三方助手的用户会发现电池寿命严重耗尽，因为第三方助手在后台无限制地运行，并在本地运行昂贵的推理以节省自己的服务器成本。

(C) 谷歌制定了所有这些，主要的人工智能助手确实支持它，并且不存在隐私丑闻，也不存在任何电池寿命问题，因为制造这些助手的每家公司在开发它们时都尊重用户隐私以及 CPU 和内存消耗等设备资源。

(D) 谷歌在欧盟将系统集成的 Gemini 从 Android 中撤下，或严格限制其功能。 与其将第三方助手从应用程序提升为系统软件，不如将 Gemini 降级为单纯应用程序的特权，让欧盟的 Android 用户无法获得系统集成的人工智能助手。

在所有情况下，Android 系统级人工智能的未来功能更新在欧盟都将出现较晚或永远不会出现。 未来的新功能不可能与世界其他地区同时在欧盟推出，因为为了符合 DMA 合规性，谷歌将需要添加对第三方助手的支持来完成同样的事情。 他们不会为等待的世界其他地方提供新功能。

鉴于谷歌去年开始在 Android 上发布 Gemini，我什至不确定委员会的指导是否允许 (D)。 整个指导文件是在这样的假设下编写的：Google 将通过构建指导要求的 API 来遵守，而不是通过删除 Gemini 系统集成功能来实现对等。 对于谷歌来说，向 Android 发布删除核心 AI 功能的更新会很尴尬，也不受欢迎，但这可能比替代方案更容易接受。1 请注意，据我记得，对于 iOS，苹果尚未从欧盟撤回任何现有功能。 他们只是拒绝或推迟了新功能。 谷歌设备上的双子座马已经走出谷仓。

最后，虽然这份指导文件适用于 Android，而不是 iOS，但我认为欧盟委员会没有理由不要求 Apple 提供全部或大部分相同的东西。 他们尚未向苹果提供任何指导，因为欧盟委员会的政策是，只有在 DMA 指定看门人运送了被裁定不合规的产品后才提供指导。 但很难想象苹果会接受其中大部分条款。 不受限制的后台处理和对麦克风、摄像头和传感器的访问？ 在硬件 DSP 上运行的第三方音频模型监听唤醒词？ 授予第三方助手不受监督地访问使用应用程序意图发布的应用程序中的所有用户数据？

不幸的是，苹果公司尚未分享任何描述其去年与欧盟委员会分享的“可信系统代理”提案的技术细节。 但无论 Apple 对可信系统代理的愿景是什么，如果 EC 想要从 Apple 获得与他们现在对 Google 的要求接近的东西，我不知道 EC 将如何认为它符合 DMA。 在本指南中，Google 没有被允许对第三方助手进行任何制衡或监督。 如果 Gemini 能做某事，那么第三方代理也一定能做。 由于 Gemini 可以在 Google 认为合适的情况下在后台运行，因此必须允许第三方助手在他们认为合适的情况下在后台运行。 如果那些第三方辅助开发者——OpenAI、Anthropic、xAI 和 Meta——在后台使用多少 CPU 合适、消耗多少 RAM 和存储、或者如何尊重用户设备上的数据方面与 Google 有不同的看法，那么这只是棘手的问题。 如果用户同意，那就可以。

一个月前，WWDC 结束后，当传言有关 Android AI 的指南即将发布时，我写道：

谷歌正在汲取苹果公司惨痛教训的教训，iOS 的所有现有功能在 DMA 生效时均被视为不符合规定。 “先发货并请求宽恕/希望它被视为合规”的策略在欧盟并不是一个好的策略。

我真的想知道欧盟委员会认为 Android 的目的是什么。 谷歌创建Android是为了自身服务的利益。 谷歌当时担心的是微软，而不是苹果，但他们希望确保自己的服务可以在主要的移动平台上使用。 我真的不明白遵守这一指导方针对谷歌来说比仅仅将系统级 Gemini 引入欧盟更具吸引力。 他们要么浪费一大笔钱构建没人会使用的 API，要么花这笔小钱构建 API 以使其最大的竞争对手受益。 我知道这是成为指定 DMA 看门人的预期价格。 但谷歌这样做的动机是什么，而不是在欧盟放弃 Android 上的系统集成人工智能呢？ 看起来竞争平台 iOS 也不会很快在欧盟提供该服务。

  1. 如果你被要求每个人都必须能够以相同的速度跑步，而你又不知道如何让慢的人跑得更快，你可以通过强迫快的人穿上加重的靴子来遵守。 这就是乌托邦平等主义倡议经常导致的结果。 ↩︎
